keys.description: Optional configuration section controlling compiler behavior and output features.
Branding configuration used for favicon/touch icon generation and brand image composition.
Configures how foreground and background images are processed, including alignment, blur, and visual effects.
Settings applied to the brand background layer during composition.
Alignment settings for positioning the background layer.
Horizontal alignment of the background brand image during composition (left/center/right).
Vertical alignment of the background brand image during composition (top/center/bottom).
Controls which output aspect ratios the background adjustments apply to.
Apply background adjustments to square icon outputs.
Apply background adjustments to wide icon outputs.
Blur intensity applied to the background brand layer during composition.
Sharp modulate overrides for the background brand layer.
Brightness multiplier for the background brand layer modulate adjustment. Use 1 for no change.
Saturation multiplier for the background brand layer modulate adjustment. Use 1 for no change.
Hue shift in degrees for the background brand layer modulate adjustment. Use 0 for no change.
Lightness multiplier for the background brand layer modulate adjustment. Use 1 for no change.
Settings applied to the brand foreground layer during composition.
Alignment settings for positioning the foreground layer.
Horizontal alignment of the foreground brand image during composition (left/center/right).
Vertical alignment of the foreground brand image during composition (top/center/bottom).
Blur intensity applied to the foreground brand layer during composition.
Sharp modulate overrides for the foreground brand layer.
Brightness multiplier for the foreground brand layer modulate adjustment. Use 1 for no change.
Saturation multiplier for the foreground brand layer modulate adjustment. Use 1 for no change.
Hue shift in degrees for the foreground brand layer modulate adjustment. Use 0 for no change.
Lightness multiplier for the foreground brand layer modulate adjustment. Use 1 for no change.
Specifies the sizes for various branding images including favicons and touch icons.
ICO size list for generating favicon.ico (multiple embedded resolutions).
List of favicon.ico layer sizes (in pixels) to generate.
Per-file output dimensions for generated favicon/touch icon images. This is a map keyed by output filename (e.g. 'apple-touch-icon-57x57.png').
A single per-file image size entry under `option.brand.size.image`. The key name is the output filename (any string). The value must be an object with `width` and `height`.
Target width (in pixels) for this generated brand image entry (keyed by filename).
Target height (in pixels) for this generated brand image entry (keyed by filename).
Cache controls for enabling and tuning incremental build behavior.
Enable the compiler cache system (allows skipping unchanged work between runs).
Filter build streams using cache so only changed inputs are processed.
Enable deterministic sorting behavior within cache-backed pipelines.
Persist cache entries to disk after successful processing.
General config-file behavior settings (for example key ordering when saving).
When writing/printing config, sort object keys for stable, deterministic output.
Highlighting-related configuration (e.g., syntax highlighting).
PrismJS and Prism Live highlighting settings (Prism assets + optional Prism Live vendored module files).
Enable/disable PrismJS syntax highlighting asset generation.
Generate prism.js and prism.scss index files to simplify importing PrismJS assets.
Prism Live asset generation settings (copies vendored files into `in/asset`, typically including Bliss + Prism Live core + selected language modules).
Enable/disable Prism Live vendored file generation.
List container for Prism Live language modules to copy (`prism-live-{id}.min.js`).
List of Prism Live language module IDs to include (`css`, `javascript`, `markup`).

Base output directory for Prism Live files (for example `module/prism-live`). Minified files such as `*.min.css` and `*.min.js` are copied directly under `in/asset/<path>`, while non-minified CSS and JS files are copied under `in/asset/css/<path>` and `in/asset/js/<path>` so they can go through the normal asset pipelines.
Language inclusion settings for PrismJS syntax highlighting.
Enable/disable PrismJS language component inclusion.
List container for PrismJS language IDs to include in generated syntax assets.
List of PrismJS language ids to include. Dropdown values are sourced from local `node_modules/prismjs/components.json`, and custom text values are also allowed.
Output directories for PrismJS assets.
Output directory for generated PrismJS CSS modules (themes/plugins).
Output directory for generated PrismJS JavaScript modules (languages/plugins).
Plugin inclusion settings for PrismJS syntax highlighting.
Enable/disable PrismJS plugin inclusion.
List container for PrismJS plugin IDs to include in generated syntax assets.
List of PrismJS plugin ids to include. Dropdown values are sourced from local `node_modules/prismjs/components.json`, and custom text values are also allowed.

Theme settings for PrismJS syntax highlighting. Any one of `theme.include`, `theme.bundle`, or `theme.default` can be set; missing values resolve by fallback chain `include -> bundle -> default -> prism`.
Enable/disable PrismJS theme inclusion.
List container for PrismJS theme IDs to include in generated syntax assets. Legacy/canonical key: when only this key is set, `theme.bundle` and `theme.default` fall back from it.
Preferred default PrismJS theme id. If omitted, resolves from `theme.include[0]`, then `theme.bundle[0]`, then `prism`.
List of PrismJS theme ids to include. Dropdown values are sourced from local `node_modules/prismjs/components.json`, and custom text values are also allowed.
List container for PrismJS theme IDs to bundle into generated `prism.scss` / `prism.min.css`. If omitted, resolves from `theme.include`, then `theme.default`, then `prism`.

List of PrismJS theme ids to bundle into the generated main Prism stylesheet. Keep this smaller than `theme.include` when pages need many selectable themes without stacking them all into `prism.min.css`. This can be the only theme key set; `include`/`default` will fall back from it.
HTML processing options (e.g., minification).
HTML minification options applied during compilation.
When minifying HTML, collapse unnecessary whitespace.
When minifying HTML, also minify inline <style> blocks / CSS.
When minifying HTML, also minify inline <script> blocks / JavaScript.
When minifying HTML, remove HTML comments.
Image processing options (minification, interlacing, variants, watermarking).
Interlace rule list (may be empty).
A single interlace rule entry.
Image minification options per optimizer/codec.
Path filtering for image minification. Include is evaluated first, then exclude. Defaults to include '**/*' and exclude [].
Glob patterns to include for image minification checks. If omitted or empty, defaults to ['**/*'].
Single include glob pattern for image minification filtering.
Glob patterns excluded from image minification after include matching.
Single exclude glob pattern for image minification filtering.
Settings container for gifsicle GIF optimization.
Image minification option 'interlaced' for gifsicle (GIF optimizer).
Settings container for jpegoptim JPEG optimization.
Image minification option 'progressive' for jpegoptim (JPEG optimizer).
Image minification option 'stripAll' for jpegoptim (JPEG optimizer).
Settings container for mozjpeg JPEG encoding/optimization.
Image minification option 'progressive' for mozjpeg (JPEG encoder).
Image minification option 'quality' for mozjpeg (JPEG encoder).
Settings container for optipng PNG optimization.
Image minification option 'optimizationLevel' for optipng (PNG optimizer).
Image minification option 'strip' for optipng (PNG optimizer).
Settings container for pngcrush PNG optimization.
Image minification option 'reduce' for pngcrush (PNG optimizer).
Image minification option 'strip' for pngcrush (PNG optimizer).
SVG optimization options via SVGO.
SVGO plugin configuration list.
SVGO plugin list applied during SVG optimization.
Whether this SVGO plugin is enabled for SVG optimization.
SVGO plugin name for this plugin entry.
Maximum parallelism for image optimization. Null/undefined typically means auto/default.
Defines generated image variants (e.g., thumbnails). Each rule matches source images and emits a suffixed resized copy. Watermark rules (if matched) are applied independently to each output image.
A single image variant rule entry.
Glob pattern used to select source images this variant rule applies to.
Variant max bound. Number form is max pixels (applies to both width and height with fit-inside). String form is a percentage of the source image max side (e.g., '25%').
Suffix inserted before extension for generated variant files (default '.thumb').
Defines a list of **watermark rules**. Each rule specifies:
A single watermark rule entry.
List of layers applied by a watermark rule (composited in order).
A single watermark layer entry.
Background color/setting used when resizing/tiling watermark layers (e.g., 'none').
Extra GraphicsMagick options passed to the composite step (e.g., -tile).
Placement of a watermark layer on the target image (GraphicsMagick composite gravity; e.g., Center, SouthEast).
Project-relative path to the watermark image file used for a watermark layer.
Opacity (percentage) for the watermark layer when composited onto the target image.
Scale factor applied to the watermark layer before compositing (1.0 = unchanged).
Glob pattern used to select which images a watermark rule applies to.
JavaScript processing options (e.g., minification).
JavaScript minification options (typically passed to the minifier).
Enable JavaScript compression optimizations during minification.
When minifying JS, preserve class names (useful for reflection/debugging).
When minifying JS, preserve function names (useful for stack traces/debugging).
Enable JavaScript identifier mangling during minification.
Markdown processing options.
Project-relative directory that contains markdown/HTML include fragments used during compilation.
Mirror rule list for copying arbitrary project files into output.
A single mirror rule entry.
Project-relative source path (file or directory) to mirror into output.
Project-relative destination directory (under out/) where mirrored files are copied.
Glob pattern(s) controlling which files under `source` are mirrored. Supports negated patterns starting with '!'.
Optional rename rule(s) applied to mirrored relative paths. String form is a single-file target path; array form is rule-based.
A single mirror rename rule object used when `option.mirrors[].rename` is an array.
Glob pattern used to match source-relative paths for this rename rule.
Regex source string used in regex mode instead of glob mode.
Regex flags (e.g., 'g', 'i') used with `regex` in regex mode.
Target relative path/filename to rename matched paths to.
Navigation feature settings (breadcrumb, ToC, shared nav).
Breadcrumb injection settings.
Base path prefix used when generating breadcrumb link URLs (e.g., to mount output under a subdirectory).
Enable/disable breadcrumb injection into generated HTML.
Optional ignore patterns for breadcrumb generation (null to use shared defaults: option.navigation.shared.ignore_patterns).
Paths/glob patterns excluded from breadcrumb generation.
HTML marker string that will be replaced with the generated breadcrumb HTML.
Label/content used for the breadcrumb root link (e.g., 'Home').
Shared navigation settings used as defaults for other nav sections.
List container for shared navigation ignore patterns.
Paths/patterns that should be excluded from shared navigation features (e.g., index/404/503 pages).
Table-of-contents (ToC) generation and injection settings.
Whether the ToC UI should be collapsible (if the template/CSS supports it).
Maximum heading depth to include in the ToC (e.g., 4 includes up to h4).
Enable/disable table-of-contents (ToC) generation/injection.
HTML inserted at the start of each ToC entry label (e.g., a counter span).
Selectors that receive header_prepend injection.
CSS class that marks headings to be excluded from the ToC.
Optional ignore patterns for ToC generation (null to use shared defaults).
Paths/glob patterns excluded from ToC generation.
HTML marker string that will be replaced with the generated ToC HTML.
Heading CSS selectors used to build the ToC (e.g., h1,h2,h3...).
Optional HTML template used to wrap emoji clusters in visible ToC heading text. Use `*` as the emoji placeholder; null disables wrapping.
Packaging settings for producing deployable bundles (zip and/or directory).
HTML-driven packaging rules (e.g., ignore classes).
List container for HTML class names used by packaging exclusion rules (default includes "no-package").
HTML class names that indicate content/pages should be excluded from package outputs (default item: "no-package").
Top-level package ignore pattern list applied across package targets (unless disabled per path, default includes "no-package").
Top-level ignore patterns applied when building package outputs (default item: "no-package").
Controls which package output formats are produced.
When packaging, also emit an unpacked directory output.
When packaging, emit a .zip archive output.
List of package target definitions.
A single package target definition.
List of source-to-destination mappings for a package target.
A single source-to-destination mapping for a package target.
Destination subdirectory inside the packaged output (zip or folder) where the source files are placed.
Per-path package ignore pattern list for this target mapping.
Ignore patterns applied within a package path mapping.
If true, disables ignore processing for this path mapping.
Project-relative source directory to include in a package target.
Suffix appended to the package name for this target (useful for variants).
Path-related settings (ignore prefixes and sitemap exclusions).
Files/directories starting with this prefix are ignored by default (commonly '_' for partials).
List container for paths/slugs excluded from sitemap generation.
Page slugs/paths to exclude from sitemap generation (commonly error pages like 404/503).
Token replacement settings (delimiters used for placeholder replacement).
Delimiter strings used to detect replacement tokens in content.
Closing delimiter for config token replacement in templates/content (e.g., '...]]').
Opening delimiter for config token replacement in templates/content (e.g., '[[...').
Automatic title generation settings.
Rules for composing titles from directory + filename/header pieces.
How many directory segments to include when composing titles. -1 typically means 'all'.
String used to join directory segments when composing titles.
Order to arrange directory segments when composing automatic document titles ('leafToRoot' reverses the directory chain; 'rootToLeaf' keeps natural order).
String used to join the directory-derived portion with the page title portion.
Rules for converting raw names (filenames/headers) into human-readable titles.
Case-handling rules used during title generation.
If true, split CamelCase/PascalCase words into separate words when generating titles.
If true, preserve original casing from the source instead of enforcing title casing rules.
Separator/regex rules for splitting and joining title words.
List container for separator characters converted to spaces before title formatting.
Characters that should be replaced with spaces before title generation (e.g., '_' and '-').
List container for regex/string patterns stripped during title formatting.
Regex/string patterns to strip from titles before final formatting.
Separator used to join words into the final title string.
Regex used to split raw text into words for title generation.
Word-level casing rules used during title generation.
List container for words that should stay uppercase in generated titles.
Acronyms that should remain uppercase when generating human-readable titles.
List container for words that should stay lowercase in generated titles.
Words that should remain lowercase in generated titles (unless first/last).
List container for words that should keep exact casing in generated titles.
Words that should use exact casing when generating titles (e.g., JavaScript).
Where automatic page titles come from: 'filename' uses the file name; 'header' uses the first <h1> in the HTML when present.
URL formatting settings (clean URLs, trailing slash).
If true, generate clean URLs (omit the .html extension).
If true, include a trailing slash on directory index URLs.
Watch-mode debounce/delay settings for change detection, dependent rebuild triggering, and browser reload.
Browsersync server settings for watch/browser tasks, including ports, HTTPS, and local TLS certificate file paths.
Enable HTTPS for Browsersync local preview. Defaults to true; when key/cert are blank Browsersync uses its own self-signed certificate.
Project-relative or absolute path to a PEM private key file for Browsersync HTTPS. Used only when both key and cert are provided.
Project-relative or absolute path to a PEM certificate file for Browsersync HTTPS. Used only when both key and cert are provided.
Browser reload debounce/delay in milliseconds after changes are detected.
Change-detection debounce/delay in milliseconds for watch mode event batching and follow-up dependent task scheduling.
Config-driven watch trigger rules. Each rule maps file match patterns and events to task ids.
Single watch trigger rule defining `match`, `events`, and `tasks`.
Glob/path patterns (project-relative or absolute) to match changed files for this trigger rule.
Single glob/path pattern for this watch trigger rule.
Event filter for this trigger rule. Supported values: add, change, unlink, addDir, unlinkDir.
Single filesystem event name used to filter this trigger rule.
Task ids to enqueue when this trigger rule matches (for example: build_html, rebuild_html).
Single task id to enqueue when this trigger rule matches.
Required configuration section. Values here must be provided for correct site generation.
Required site metadata used for URLs, titles, and generated tags.
Default site title used for HTML <title> and metadata when generating pages.
Base URL of the website, used for canonical/og:url meta tags and sitemap absolute links.
